title: OpenClaw Public Baseline Recommendation
description: Recommend which public OpenClaw surface should anchor Golem's next public-facing planning cycle while browser execution stays on the accepted sidecar lane.
question: Which public OpenClaw surface should Golem use as the baseline for the next public-facing planning cycle while browser execution stays on the accepted sidecar lane?

sources.label: oc_cli
sources.notes: Primary CLI reference with command tree, status and gateway surface.

sources.label: oc_channels
sources.notes: Public channel surface and routing overview.

sources.label: oc_browser
sources.notes: Public browser docs, including managed browser, existing-session and Playwright prerequisites.

sources.label: oc_plugins
sources.notes: Public plugin architecture and extension surface.

decision_criteria.label: Operational Command Baseline
decision_criteria.description: The baseline should expose a broad public command surface for day-to-day orientation and verification.
decision_criteria.notes: Favor surfaces that make ongoing operational work visible.

decision_criteria.label: Public Workflow Reach
decision_criteria.description: The baseline should cover public-facing workflows that Golem can actually observe and reason about from docs alone.
decision_criteria.notes: Prefer public surfaces that describe real-world message or routing workflows.

decision_criteria.label: Extension Follow-on Path
decision_criteria.description: The baseline should leave a visible path to later extension work without becoming a plugin deep-dive by default.
decision_criteria.notes: This matters, but it is secondary to day-one baseline utility.

decision_criteria.label: Browser Surface Specificity
decision_criteria.description: The baseline should still make browser-specific public claims visible enough to track them as a secondary lane.
decision_criteria.notes: Keeps browser docs in view without forcing them to become the main baseline.

alternatives.label: CLI + Channels Baseline
alternatives.description: Anchor the next planning cycle on the CLI reference plus the public channels surface.
alternatives.intended_outcome: Use CLI and channels docs as the default public baseline for near-term planning and verification work.
alternatives.relative_cost_note: Low relative cost because the surface is broad, public and already oriented around operational commands and channels.
risk_hints: Can underrepresent extension mechanics and plugin-specific growth paths.
Does not by itself settle browser implementation questions.
preconditions: Keep browser execution on the accepted sidecar lane instead of treating OpenClaw browser docs as a runtime contract.
Treat plugin work as a follow-on surface, not the default baseline.
alternatives.suggested_next_step: Use CLI + channels as the public baseline for the next project tranche, and keep browser-native work frozen until a separate evidence-backed case justifies reopening it.
alternatives.notes: Best fit when the project needs a public baseline for day-to-day planning instead of a browser-specific deep dive.

alternatives.label: Browser Surface Baseline
alternatives.description: Center the next planning cycle on the public browser docs plus CLI support material.
alternatives.intended_outcome: Treat browser docs as the primary baseline for near-term planning.
alternatives.relative_cost_note: Higher relative cost because the public browser surface carries more prerequisites, profile modes and implementation-specific caveats.
risk_hints: Can over-focus the project on a surface with many prerequisites and branch conditions.
May tempt the project to re-litigate browser-native work before there is a new evidence threshold.
preconditions: Accept the public browser docs as a planning surface without treating them as proof of host readiness.
Track Playwright, profile and existing-session prerequisites explicitly.
alternatives.suggested_next_step: Only elevate browser docs to the main baseline if the project explicitly decides to reopen browser-native work with fresh public and local evidence.
alternatives.notes: Useful for browser-specific follow-up, but heavy as a general baseline.

alternatives.label: Plugin Extension Baseline
alternatives.description: Anchor the next planning cycle on plugin architecture and extension surfaces with CLI support as context.
alternatives.intended_outcome: Use plugin docs as the default public baseline for what to build next.
alternatives.relative_cost_note: Medium relative cost because extension work is publicly documented but assumes extra configuration and restart flows.
risk_hints: Can skew planning toward extensibility before the project chooses the right operational baseline.
May pull attention away from already-proven public workflows.
preconditions: Decide that near-term project work is primarily about extension or integration design.
Accept Gateway restart and plugin config workflows as part of the baseline.
alternatives.suggested_next_step: Use plugin docs as a secondary planning surface once the operational baseline is stable and the project is ready to scope extension work explicitly.
alternatives.notes: Stronger as a secondary lane than as the first baseline.
